skill.name: embedding-strategies
skill.description: Select embedding models, chunking methods, and evaluation metrics for semantic search and RAG applications.
skill.summary: Plan and evaluate embedding strategies for semantic search, RAG, and domain-specific retrieval.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static external command, weak cryptography, and system reconnaissance alerts were false positives caused by Markdown syntax or ordinary embedding guidance. The meaningful risk is that example code can send document text to external embedding services, which needs user awareness for sensitive data.

medium_findings.title: External Embedding API Receives Document Text
medium_findings.description: The OpenAI embedding example sends each text batch to an external embedding API. This is expected for hosted embeddings, but users must avoid sending secrets, regulated data, or private documents without approval.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The examples directly create embeddings from caller-provided text. The behavior is legitimate for this skill, but it creates a clear privacy and data-transfer risk.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Alerts Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick locations are Markdown fences or inline documentation references. No evidence found of shell command execution or command injection behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are visible Markdown code block delimiters or inline file references. There is no executable shell syntax in the skill file at these locations.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL Alerts Are Reference Links
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs point to OpenAI, Sentence Transformers, and MTEB documentation resources. They are not used for hidden network calls or data exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs appear only in the Resources section as Markdown links. No code path uses these links to transmit local data.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are Terminology False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts appear to be triggered by embedding-related text, not cryptographic code. No evidence found of MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or similar weak algorithms.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines describe embedding model selection and link to documentation. They do not contain cryptographic algorithm usage.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Alerts Are Benign Data Handling
low_findings.description: The cited lines assign document identifiers and recommend caching embeddings. No evidence found of host enumeration, credential discovery, or system reconnaissance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are part of document-processing examples and best practices. They do not inspect the operating system, network, or environment.

dangerous_patterns.title: Hosted Embedding Call With User Supplied Text
dangerous_patterns.description: The example builds embeddings from arbitrary text input using a hosted model call. This pattern can expose sensitive source content if users apply it to private corpora without controls.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The API call is direct and semantically tied to user-provided text batches. The risk depends on the data being embedded, not on malicious skill behavior.

content.value_statement: Embedding choices affect retrieval quality, latency, and cost. This skill guides model selection, chunking, domain pipelines, and quality evaluation for RAG systems.
actual_capabilities: Compare embedding models by dimensions, token limits, and common use cases.
Suggest chunking strategies for token, sentence, markdown, and recursive splitting.
Outline OpenAI and local sentence-transformer embedding workflows.
Design domain-specific pipelines for documents and code search.
Evaluate retrieval quality with precision, recall, MRR, NDCG, and similarity metrics.
limitations: It provides guidance and templates, not a complete production vector database.
Model details and benchmark rankings may change after the skill was written.
Hosted embedding examples may send text to external providers.
Local model examples require separate dependencies and hardware planning.

use_cases.title: Plan a RAG Retrieval Layer
use_cases.description: Choose an embedding model, chunk size, overlap, and evaluation method for a new RAG application.

use_cases.title: Improve Search Quality
use_cases.description: Review an existing semantic search pipeline and tune chunking, preprocessing, and similarity metrics.

use_cases.title: Select Local or Hosted Embeddings
use_cases.description: Compare privacy, cost, latency, and accuracy tradeoffs before choosing hosted APIs or local models.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Model
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me choose an embedding model for a RAG app. My content is [content type], my budget is [budget], and my latency target is [target].

prompt_templates.title: Design Chunking
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a chunking strategy for [document type]. Include chunk size, overlap, boundary rules, metadata, and expected retrieval tradeoffs.

prompt_templates.title: Evaluate Retrieval
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an embedding evaluation plan for [application]. Include test queries, relevance labels, metrics, and success thresholds.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize a Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this embedding pipeline design: [pipeline details]. Identify risks, privacy concerns, model mismatches, and optimization steps.

output_examples.input: I need embeddings for a support knowledge base with short articles and strict cost limits.
output_examples.output: Recommended direction: use a cost-effective hosted embedding model, chunk by section headings, keep moderate overlap, store article metadata, and evaluate top-k recall against real support questions.

output_examples.input: Our code search results miss function-level matches.
output_examples.output: Recommended direction: chunk by functions and classes, include surrounding file context, test a code-focused embedding model, and measure retrieval with developer-authored search queries.

output_examples.input: We need multilingual search over policy documents.
output_examples.output: Recommended direction: choose a multilingual embedding model, preserve section structure, normalize text carefully, and evaluate queries in each supported language.

best_practices: Match the embedding model to content type, language needs, privacy rules, and latency targets.
Preserve semantic boundaries when chunking, then measure retrieval quality with labeled examples.
Document provider choices and avoid sending sensitive data to hosted services without approval.
anti_patterns: Do not mix embeddings from different models in the same vector space.
Do not optimize chunk size without measuring retrieval quality on real queries.
Do not treat public benchmarks as a replacement for domain-specific evaluation.

faq.question: Does this skill build a vector database?
faq.answer: No. It guides embedding model selection, chunking, and evaluation for systems that may use a vector database.

faq.question: Can it help with OpenAI embeddings?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for OpenAI embedding workflows and dimension reduction choices.

faq.question: Can it support local embedding models?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers sentence-transformer style local models and the tradeoffs of running them.

faq.question: Is it safe for sensitive documents?
faq.answer: Use caution. Hosted embedding examples can send document text to external providers.

faq.question: What quality metrics does it cover?
faq.answer: It covers precision, recall, mean reciprocal rank, normalized discounted cumulative gain, and similarity comparisons.

faq.question: Can Claude, Codex, or Claude Code use it?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
